external_narratives.summary: Account of the mid-air collision on 23 June 1944 during 97 Squadron formation flying practice from RAF Coningsby. Lancaster ND981 OF-T piloted by Fl/Lt Ted Perkins had its tail removed when ME625 dropped onto it from above. The aircraft broke apart at 1,000 ft; Sgt Coman bailed out and survived.
key_details: Six Lancasters in two V formations
ND981 tail removed by collision from above
Crash witnessed by villagers at Crowland fete

external_narratives.summary: Account of Lancaster ME625 OF-O piloted by Australian Fl/Lt Van Raalte. The aircraft became trapped in the slipstream of the lead Lancaster during a gentle turn, causing the fatal collision. ME625 crashed two miles from ND981 with no survivors.
key_details: ME625 crashed two miles from ND981
